meta.title: AI SDK Extension Refactoring — Codex Provider Architecture
meta.scope: AI SDK 擴充重構 + codex 進階效能 + 遺留清理

A-0.name: Refactor Codex Provider Architecture
controls: C1: AGENTS.md Rule 1 — No silent fallback
C2: AI SDK pipeline must remain intact (25K lines)
C3: Existing functionality must not regress
C4: Graceful degradation — server unsupported features must not break
outputs: O1: Codex provider with 5 Responses API optimizations active
O2: WebSocket transport adapter (fetch interceptor level)
O3: Cleaned codebase (dead code removed, duplication resolved)
O4: Updated plan documents (design.md, tasks.md, idef0.json, grafcet.json)
mechanisms: M1: AI SDK streamText pipeline (7 transform stages)
M4: OpenAI Responses API server-side features

A1.name: Inject Provider Options Into AI SDK Path
inputs: I1: AI SDK providerOptions schema (17+ fields verified)
I2: Current llm.ts streamText() call site
controls: C1: OPENAI_REQUEST_OPTION_KEYS whitelist in transform.ts
C2: AI SDK adapter auto-compose logic (store=false → include encrypted_content)
outputs: O1: providerOptions.openai.store = false active
O2: providerOptions.openai.serviceTier = 'priority' active
O3: providerOptions.openai.promptCacheKey injectable from session
O4: providerOptions.openai.previousResponseId injectable from session

A11.name: Activate Store And Service Tier
controls: C1: Only for codex/codex-subscription-* providers
outputs: O1: store=false → encrypted reasoning auto-included
O2: serviceTier=priority → Pro queue routing

A12.name: Migrate Cache Key To Provider Options
inputs: I1: Session-stable cache key (currently in fetch interceptor)
controls: C1: Fetch interceptor fallback retained for safety
outputs: O1: Cache key set via providerOptions (AI SDK constructs body)

A13.name: Track Response Identity For Delta
inputs: I1: AI SDK providerMetadata.responseId from finish event
controls: C1: Only capture when codex provider active
outputs: O1: previousResponseId available for next turn
O2: Delta eligibility flag (instructions+tools unchanged)

A2.name: Extend Fetch Interceptor Body Transform
inputs: I1: AI SDK constructed request body (from @ai-sdk/openai adapter)
I2: Codex-specific fields not in AI SDK (context_management)
controls: C1: Additive transform only — never overwrite AI SDK fields
C2: Body transform runs AFTER AI SDK constructs body
outputs: O1: context_management field injected
O2: Fetch interceptor duplication cleaned (instructions, prompt_cache_key)

A21.name: Add Context Management Injection
controls: C1: Only inject for codex endpoint requests
outputs: O1: body.context_management = [{type:'compaction', compact_threshold: N}]

A22.name: Deduplicate Fetch Interceptor Logic
inputs: I3: Identified duplications (instructions, headers, token refresh, body transform)
controls: C1: CodexNativeAuthPlugin is strict superset of CodexAuthPlugin
outputs: O1: Single consolidated auth+fetch logic
O2: instructions extraction removed (AI SDK adapter handles via providerOptions)
O3: prompt_cache_key fallback simplified

A3.name: Build WebSocket Transport Adapter
inputs: I1: AI SDK fetch(url, {body, headers}) call
I3: Old codex-websocket.ts (reference: delta detection, handshake)
controls: C1: AI SDK must not know transport is WebSocket (transparent adapter)
C2: HTTP fallback mandatory on WS failure
C3: One WS connection = one active request (no multiplexing)
outputs: O1: Synthetic SSE Response wrapping WS events
O2: Incremental delta (previous_response_id + delta input)
O3: Persistent WS connection (per-session reuse)
mechanisms: M2: codex.ts fetch interceptor (transport switch point)

A31.name: Manage WebSocket Connection
inputs: I1: First codex request in session
controls: C1: Connection state machine: idle → connecting → open → failed
C2: Per-session isolation (no shared WS across sessions)
outputs: O1: Open WS connection with proper handshake headers
O2: Connection state for transport decision

A32.name: Transform WebSocket Events To SSE Stream
inputs: I1: WS text frames (JSONL events from codex server)
controls: C1: AI SDK SSE parser expects 'data: {json}\n\n' format
C2: Stream must close on response.completed/failed/error
outputs: O1: ReadableStream of SSE-formatted text

A33.name: Construct Synthetic HTTP Response
controls: C1: Response must pass AI SDK's response validation
outputs: O1: Response object with SSE body that AI SDK consumes transparently

A34.name: Compute Incremental Delta
inputs: I1: Current request body (full input array)
controls: C1: Delta only when instructions+tools unchanged
C2: Full history when any non-input field changed
outputs: O1: Delta request (previous_response_id + new input items only — trimmed by fetch interceptor)
O2: Full request (when delta not applicable)
mechanisms: M1: Fetch interceptor input slice (AI SDK adapter does NOT auto-delta)

A4.name: Integrate Server Side Compaction
inputs: I1: Conversation history approaching context limit
controls: C1: Only for codex provider
C2: Fallback to client-side compaction on server failure
outputs: O1: Compacted conversation history (>50% token reduction)
O2: Compaction event logged

A5.name: Remove Dead Code And Resolve Conflicts
inputs: I2: Unsafe type casts (llm.ts, provider.ts, compaction.ts)
I3: Duplicated auth plugins (CodexAuthPlugin vs CodexNativeAuthPlugin)
controls: C1: No regression — all existing tests must pass
C2: Type safety — remove all (as any) casts for dead methods
outputs: O1: codex-language-model.ts removed or gutted (816 lines)
O2: codex-websocket.ts removed (574 lines)
O3: CUSTOM_LOADER branch in provider.ts removed
O4: setAuth/setCompactThreshold/setCompactedOutput calls removed
O5: Turn state moved from module-level to per-session storage
mechanisms: M2: llm.ts unsafe type cast sites

A51.name: Remove CUSTOM LOADER Infrastructure
controls: C1: Verify no runtime imports remain
outputs: O1: 1,390+ lines of dead code removed
mechanisms: M1: grep for all import/require references

A52.name: Remove Unsafe Type Casts
controls: C1: TypeScript strict mode compliance
outputs: O1: Clean type-safe LLM call path

A53.name: Isolate Turn State Per Session
controls: C1: Session isolation — no cross-session state leakage
outputs: O1: Per-session turn state storage
mechanisms: M1: Session storage or WeakMap keyed by session ID
